description:
You are a compliance officer who must create a point-in-time process-inventory snapshot for your workstation.  
  Carry out the following steps strictly in the order given.

  1. Background reference process  
     • Start a harmless background process that is easy to recognise: /bin/sleep 30  
       (run it so that it immediately detaches and keeps running long enough to be captured).

  2. Create a dedicated workspace  
     • Make sure the directory /home/user/compliance exists (create it if needed).

  3. Capture the snapshot  
     • Produce a CSV-style list of every process that is running **under your username only** at the moment of capture.  
     • Use the exact five columns, in the exact order, with the exact heading shown below (case-sensitive, no extra spaces):  
       PID,USER,%CPU,%MEM,COMMAND  
     • Omit all additional headers, footers, or blank lines supplied by the ps utility.  
     • Sort the resulting list numerically by PID in ascending order.

  4. Persist the result  
     • Save the file to /home/user/compliance/process_audit.csv  
     • The file must contain:
         line 1: the required header  
         line 2-n: one row per running process that belongs to your user.  
       At least one of the data rows **must** correspond to the sleep 30 process you started in step 1 – its COMMAND field will therefore literally be “sleep”.

  5. Do not print the entire CSV to the terminal; just a short success confirmation message is fine.  

  The automated checker will verify:  
  • /home/user/compliance/process_audit.csv exists and is readable.  
  • The first line of the file is exactly: PID,USER,%CPU,%MEM,COMMAND  
  • The file contains ≥ 2 lines (header + at least one data row).  
  • At least one data row ends with “,sleep”.  
  Follow best shell-practice (use as few commands as necessary—no more than three main commands are required to meet all conditions).
truth:
The grader will:
  1. Check that the directory /home/user/compliance exists.
  2. Check that the file /home/user/compliance/process_audit.csv exists.
  3. Read the file and verify:
     • Line 1 == "PID,USER,%CPU,%MEM,COMMAND"
     • Total line count ≥ 2.
     • Regex /,sleep$/ matches at least one non-header line.
  Nothing else about other process lines is validated, so differing PIDs or additional processes will not cause failure.
